item.title: Here's why Trump is backing Ken Paxton over John Cornyn in Texas GOP Senate primary
item.description: Sen. John Cornyn is facing Trump-backed Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton in the Lone Star State's runoff for the Republican Senate nomination. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe reports from Austin.

item.title: Trump says physical exam went "perfectly" as details on Iran talks remain scarce
item.description: Following his third physical in 13 months on Tuesday, President Trump said his health is perfect. The exam comes a day after the U.S. launched "self-defense strikes" in southern Iran. CBS News' Nancy Cordes and Aaron MacLean have the latest.

item.title: Meet the 2026 World Cup U.S. Men's National Team
item.description: The squad was announced during an event in New York City on Tuesday after U.S. Men's National Team manager Mauricio Pochettino spent months evaluating players to finalize the roster.

item.title: Trump administration proposes NDAs for all federal employees to curb leaks
item.description: The Trump administration plans to crack down on press leaks by requiring new and existing federal employees to sign NDAs.

item.title: U.S. Men's National Team World Cup roster announced
item.description: The U.S. has officially unveiled its World Cup roster. CBS News correspondent Nicole Valdes spoke with some of the players and has the latest.

item.title: Doctor breaks down social media menopause myths
item.description: Many women struggling with menopause symptoms can find advice on social media claiming to offer relief, but many of these tips are unproven. Dr. Kathleen Jordan, chief medical officer at Midi Health, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Iran says U.S. violating ceasefire as Trump seeks "good deal or no deal"
item.description: As Iran accuses the U.S. of a "grave violation" of the fragile ceasefire, Rubio says a deal is still possible despite the latest clash.

item.title: Study evaluates how accurate AI is on news and current events: "There's a lot of work to be done"
item.description: More than 1 billion people worldwide use artificial intelligence chatbots, but just how accurate are the answers? Forum AI released results from its latest study to determine the accuracy of chatbots in news and current events. Robbie Goldfarb, co-founder of Forum AI, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Deaths reported after chemical tank implodes at Washington mill
item.description: Nippon Dynawave Packaging Co. and local law enforcement said the tank's rupture caused "multiple critical injuries" as well as fatalities.

item.title: SPLC seeks dismissal of criminal charges, saying prosecution is vindictive
item.description: The Southern Poverty Law Center asked a federal judge to dismiss the criminal charges filed against it by the Justice Department, saying the indictment represents a "top-down, retributive campaign" directed by President Trump.

item.title: Trump moves Cabinet meeting from Camp David to White House due to weather
item.description: All Cabinet members, including outgoing Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard, were expected to attend the Camp David meeting, a White House official told CBS News.

item.title: Stocks mixed as investors shrug off renewed fighting in Iran
item.description: Oil prices were also mixed after U.S. strikes on Iranian forces, underscoring the risks still hanging over markets and consumers.

item.title: New Jersey Gov. Mikie Sherrill denied access to Newark ICE facility
item.description: Protests continue to escalate outside of an immigration detention facility in Newark, New Jersey. CBS News legal reporter Katrina Kaufman has the details.

item.title: BP ousts recently appointed chair over "conduct issues"
item.description: The board upheaval comes less than a year after BP appointed Albert Manifold to the role.

item.title: Kevin Warsh is now leading the Fed. His main challenge is a doozy.
item.description: Warsh is taking over as Fed chair as the U.S. faces the hottest inflation in years, impeding the interest rate cuts that President Trump has demanded.

item.title: What to know before choosing a debt relief strategy
item.description: Debt relief can reduce what you owe, but the wrong strategy could also cost you more than you ultimately save.

item.title: Where to keep a $40,000 emergency fund right now (and where not to)
item.description: Have $40,000 saved for an emergency? Here are two places to consider storing it (and two places to avoid).

item.title: 4 people killed as train slams into school minibus in Belgium
item.description: Authorities in Belgium say a train slammed into a school minibus at a crossing in the town of Buggenhout, killing at least 4 people including 2 children.

item.title: South Carolina Senate rejects Trump's call to redraw congressional map
item.description: The South Carolina Senate has rejected President Trump's push to redraw the state's congressional districts in hopes Republicans could gain an extra seat.

item.title: Breaking down the wider situation in the Middle East as U.S. and Iran exchange fire
item.description: Eric Lob, associate professor for politics and international relations at Florida International University, joins CBS News to discuss the latest developments between the U.S. and Iran.

item.title: Trump undergoes "6 month physical" at Walter Reed
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday underwent a "6 month physical" at Walter Reed National Military Hospital, he posted on social media.

item.title: Infrared camera on sailboat may hold clues in Lynette Hooker investigation
item.description: CBS News has learned the sailboat used by Brian and Lynette Hooker before her disappearance in the Bahamas had an infrared camera capable of detecting heat radiation.

item.title: U.S.-wanted nephew of drug lord "El Chapo" is captured in Mexico
item.description: The nephew of notorious Mexican drug lord Joaquin "El Chapo" Guzman is sought by U.S. authorities, officials said.

item.title: Suspect arrested in North Carolina double murder nearly 2 decades later
item.description: The suspect in an infamous North Carolina double murder was arrested after nearly two decades, some 3,000 miles from the scene of the killings, police said.

item.title: DHS memo directs ICE to ramp up asylum-related fraud cases
item.description: A top DHS official directed ICE attorneys to aggressively pursue administrative fraud cases against immigration lawyers accused of filing false asylum claims.

item.title: North Korea fires ballistic missile, other weapons toward sea, South says
item.description: North Korea launched multiple short-range ballistic missiles toward the sea, South Korea's military said, the latest in a series of weapons demonstrations by Pyongyang this year.

item.title: South Korean Starbucks boss apologizes for ad campaign that evoked massacre
item.description: South Korean Starbucks' boss apologized again as it faced a backlash over a marketing campaign widely seen as mocking victims of a bloody military crackdown in 1980.

item.title: Pope Leo calls for "disarming" of AI in technology-focused encyclical
item.description: In his "Magnifica Humanitas" encyclical, Pope Leo warns that as civilization grapples with the power of AI, the main challenge is remaining "profoundly human."

item.title: Inside Iran's online propaganda war
item.description: An online propaganda war is currently being waged between the United States and Iran, and global reaction suggests the Iranians are beating the Americans at their own game.

item.title: How much emergency savings should you keep while paying off debt
item.description: It can be tricky to save for an emergency fund while paying off debt. Here's how to strike the right balance.
